xEnrich 0.1.0

Classify selected pathways after GSEA-based redundancy selection

Source: R/classify_gsea_selection.R
classify_gsea_selection.Rd

Adds interpretive classification columns to the output of run_gsea_redundancy_selection, giving the same marginal_strength, signal_class, and action structure as classify_assoc_selection for Lane A3.

classify_gsea_selection(
  sel_result,
  p_strong = 0.001,
  p_moderate = 0.01,
  ratio_primary = 0.7,
  ratio_independent = 0.5,
  ratio_partial = 0.35
)

Arguments

sel_result

Data frame returned by run_gsea_redundancy_selection.

p_strong

Numeric. p-value ceiling for "strong" signal. Default 0.001.

p_moderate

Numeric. p-value ceiling for "moderate" signal. Default 0.01.

ratio_primary

Numeric in (0, 1). Minimum redundancy_ratio for "primary" class (requires p_value <= p_strong). Default 0.70.

ratio_independent

Numeric in (0, 1). Minimum redundancy_ratio for "independent" class. Default 0.50.

ratio_partial

Numeric in (0, 1). Minimum redundancy_ratio for "partial" class (below = "redundant"). Default 0.35.

Value

The input data frame with three additional columns:

marginal_strength

Ordered factor: "strong", "moderate", "weak". Based on p_value.

signal_class

Ordered factor: "primary", "independent", "partial", "redundant". Note: no "suppressor" class (see Details).

action

Character. Plain-language reporting recommendation matching the vocabulary of classify_assoc_selection.

Signal strength in GSEA context

Lane A2 uses the MI z-score from run_info_assoc as the signal strength axis. Lane A3 does not have a z-score, but GSEA p-values serve the same role: they reflect how confidently the enrichment score exceeds the permutation null. Thresholds are expressed directly on p-value rather than on a transformed scale, making them easier to calibrate without reference to dataset-specific z-score distributions.

The signed enrichment score (enrichment_score) is preserved in the output so the user can distinguish up- from down-enriched pathways, but it does not affect classification — classification is based on independence (redundancy ratio) and statistical confidence (p-value).

Relationship to classify_assoc_selection()

The classification logic is identical to classify_assoc_selection. The only differences are:

  • Signal strength uses p_value instead of z-score. p_strong corresponds to z_strong, p_moderate to z_moderate.

  • There is no "suppressor" class. Suppressors arise from residual orthogonalisation in sample space (run_assoc_redundancy_selection). GSEA redundancy selection operates in gene space (leading edges), where this artifact does not occur.

The output columns have the same names so downstream code (tables, figures, vignettes) can treat both lanes identically.

Threshold guidance

Defaults correspond to conventional significance levels:

  • p_strong = 0.001: result is robust, the permutation null is clearly exceeded.

  • p_moderate = 0.01: reliable signal, sensitive to permutation count and dataset size.

  • p_value > p_moderate: weak evidence, report with caution.

Adjust redundancy thresholds the same way as in classify_assoc_selection: raise ratio_independent to 0.6 for GO terms (many overlapping annotations), raise ratio_primary to 0.8 for conservative reporting.

See also

run_gsea_redundancy_selection for the upstream selection step, classify_assoc_selection for the equivalent Lane A2 function, plot_gains to visualise the selection profile.

Examples

set.seed(1)
universe   <- paste0("G", 1:1000)
gene_stats <- setNames(rnorm(1000), universe)
gene_stats[universe[1:70]]    <- gene_stats[universe[1:70]]    + 4
gene_stats[universe[500:530]] <- gene_stats[universe[500:530]] + 3

gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A1 = universe[1:60],
  pathway_A2 = c(universe[1:55], universe[61:65]),
  pathway_B1 = universe[500:530],
  pathway_N1 = sample(universe[200:400], 40)
)

batch <- run_batch_gsea(
  gene_sets  = gene_sets,
  gene_stats = gene_stats,
  n_perm     = 500L,
  seed       = 1L
)
#> Running GSEA on 4 pathways [adaptive = TRUE, mode = 'refine', nthreads = 1].
#> Done. 3 / 4 pathways significant at padj <= 0.05.
#> Refine mode: 4/4 pathways resolved at 500 perms; 0 borderline pathways refined (0 extra perms).

sel <- run_gsea_redundancy_selection(
  gsea_results = batch$results,
  gene_stats   = gene_stats,
  alpha        = 0.05,
  seed         = 1L
)
#> 3 / 4 pathways significant at alpha = 0.05.
#> Leading-edge union: 91 genes (9.1% of universe).
#> Computing IT enrichment scores on leading edges...
#> Running CMI redundancy selection on leading edges...
#> Auto-selected min_gain = 0.03502 bits (noise floor = 0.00391, relevance threshold = 0.03502 [15% of leading MI]).

classified <- classify_gsea_selection(sel)
print(classified[, c("pathway", "marginal_strength",
                      "signal_class", "action")])
#>               pathway marginal_strength signal_class              action
#> pathway_A1 pathway_A1          moderate  independent report as secondary
#> pathway_B1 pathway_B1          moderate  independent report as secondary
#> pathway_A2 pathway_A2          moderate    redundant             dismiss
